GPT-5.4 just scored 75% on computer use benchmarks -- beating the human score of 72.4%.

OpenAI released GPT-5.4 in March 2026, and it's the first time a general-purpose model has computer use baked directly into its architecture. No plugins. No workarounds. It can write code to operate your computer, issue mouse and keyboard commands, and handle multi-step workflows across software environments.

The model also ships with a 1.1M token context window -- enough to fit entire codebases in one conversation. And it comes in five variants (Standard, Thinking, Pro, Mini, Nano) starting at $2.50 per million input tokens.

On GDPval, the benchmark measuring economically valuable task completion, it scored 83% -- above average professional performance on knowledge work.

This is the clearest signal yet that the line between "AI assistant" and "AI worker" just got blurred.

The $699 device that overheated in 3 hours started a revolution it never got to finish.
In 2023, Humane dropped the AI Pin and the world laughed. In 2024, Rabbit tried again — 100,000 pre-orders, then silence. But Meta's Ray-Ban glasses quietly became the first AI wearable people actually kept wearing.

HP paid $116M for Humane's patents. That alone tells you how much the underlying idea was always worth.
